home.hero_subtitle: We analyse Dutch parliamentary plenary sessions using AI — automatically extracting summaries, speaker stances, and source references to make democratic debate more accessible and transparent.
home.feature_summaries_body: Neutral 300–500 word summaries of each plenary session, generated from the full session transcript.
home.feature_stances_body: Classified positions — support, oppose, or unclear — per speaker per debate topic, backed by evidence from the transcript.
home.feature_sources_body: Every summary and stance links back to the original transcript utterances so readers can verify every claim.

chat.gate_description: Enter the access code to start chatting about this session.
chat.error_wrong_password: Incorrect access code. Please try again.
chat.input_placeholder: Ask a question about this session…

about.title: About This Project
about.p1: The Parliamentary Session Intelligence platform is a civic-tech initiative aimed at making the work of the Dutch parliament more accessible to citizens, researchers, and journalists.
about.p2: Using large language models, we automatically process transcripts from Tweede Kamer plenary sessions — producing neutral summaries, extracting speaker stances per debate topic, and linking every insight back to the original transcript segment for full verifiability.
about.p3: The platform is built on a modern open-source stack and is designed for transparency, accuracy, and ease of use.
